headline: AI Moves From Pilots to Production at the World's Largest Developer Event
summary: WeAreDevelopers World Congress brought 15,000 attendees, more than 500 speakers and technology leaders from NVIDIA, Amazon, Microsoft, Google Cloud, Atlassian, SAP, Salesforce and IBM to Berlin, under the patronage of Germany's Federal Ministry for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation. headline: Is Adobe Stock Really Broken, or Just Violently Marked Down?

headline: We’re Bullish on Adobe Despite 40% Decline From Peak Levels
summary: Adobe has lost nearly 40% of its value over the past year while its underlying business keeps hitting records and raising guidance. Something in that math does not add up, and figuring out which side is wrong could define the trade.

content.title: We’re Bullish on Adobe Despite 40% Decline From Peak Levels
content.summary: Adobe has lost nearly 40% of its value over the past year while its underlying business keeps hitting records and raising guidance. Something in that math does not add up, and figuring out which side is wrong could define the trade.

headline: Forget Palantir as It Bounces Back and Get in Salesforce Before Wall Street Wakes Up to Real Value
summary: While retail traders rush back into a stock priced like a lottery ticket, one overlooked mega-cap is quietly printing record cash flow and beating earnings estimates by double digits. The valuation gap between hype and reality has rarely been this wide.

content.title: Forget Palantir as It Bounces Back and Get in Salesforce Before Wall Street Wakes Up to Real Value

headline: Tech Stocks Slide. South Korea’s Market Nosedive Weighs.
summary: The Nasdaq was down 0.8% while the S&P 500 dropped 0.2%.  The Dow was up 0.3%, or 173 points, led by Salesforce and Chevron.  Memory chip maker SK Hynix plunged more than 15%, its largest intraday decline on record, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

content.title: Tech Stocks Slide. South Korea’s Market Nosedive Weighs.
content.summary: The Nasdaq was down 0.8% while the S&P 500 dropped 0.2%.  The Dow was up 0.3%, or 173 points, led by Salesforce and Chevron.  Memory chip maker SK Hynix plunged more than 15%, its largest intraday decline on record, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

headline: The AI Trade Has Flip Flopped (At Least for Today)
summary: Well, today software stocks are back from the dead.  The index was down 0.7% with some of the year-to-date top performers among the top laggards.  The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF was up 0.8%, compared to the PHLX Semiconductor Index which was down 4.6%.

headline: How a Major U.S. Air Force Fleet Deal At Salesforce (CRM) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: Earlier this month, Salesforce announced that the U.S. Air Force’s 441st Vehicle Support Chain Operations Squadron is using its Missionforce National Security platform on Government Cloud Plus Defense to manage a US$13.50 billion fleet of more than 84,000 vehicles across nearly 389 locations. This deployment not only replaces a legacy system with an IL5-authorized, always-on logistics backbone, it also positions the Air Force for future AI applications such as predictive maintenance,...
